response.justification: Candidate has relevant geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and a track record with low-latency, high-throughput systems via DLang at Dunnhumby, but the C++ exposure is brief (~7 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015) and dated, with no Rust experience. Recent work is firmly in the TypeScript/Node.js web stack rather than systems-level pipeline engineering, so the core 'Modern C++ or Rust' requirement is only weakly met. Worth a screening conversation to gauge depth of systems thinking and willingness to ramp on C++, but not a strong on-paper match for a senior routing-tiles role.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience through GISCollective and Green Map
Built low-latency, high-throughput services with async I/O and fibers at Dunnhumby (systems-style work in DLang)
Strong testing/quality discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) aligned with rigorous QA expectations
concerns: C++ experience is limited (~7 months) and over a decade old; no Rust experience listed
Recent 5+ years are TypeScript/Node.js web and pricing work, not systems or data-pipeline engineering
No direct experience with navigation/routing tiles, map data pipelines, or distributed-system primitives at production scale
